Appearances: Arkady Itkin of the Law Office of A. Itkin (415.640.6768) counsel for plaintiff and moving to be relieved. No other appearances.

Discussed and the Court grants Mr. Itkin's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Ilya Berin. Order signed. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael J. Doland, Esq., Imai, Tadlock, et al, 415-675-7000 appearing for Petitioner;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL LOPEZ'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT.

Defendant Lopez's motion to vacate the judgment entered on September 24, 2018 is granted. "Code of Civil Procedure section 594, subdivision (a) provides that a trial may not take place unless the adverse party was given at least 15 days' notice. This requirement is mandatory and jurisdictional; a judgment entered following a trial conducted in violation of the requirement is void." (Urethane Foam Experts, Inc. v. Latimer (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 763, 767; accord, Au-Yang v. Barton (1999) 21 Cal.4th 958, 963.) In this case, the court's notice of time and place of trial was not mailed to Lopez's correct address; the court mistakenly served Lopez's former counsel. (Lopez Dec., 3; Lopez's RJN, Ex. 2.) In addition, plaintiff did not provide proper ex parte notice of his application to continue the trial date. Plaintiff mail-served that notice only 2 days before the hearing to the wrong address. Therefore, the record unequivocally establishes that Lopez did not receive proper notice of the trial and the motion must be granted.

The parties may contact department 206 to obtain a new trial date.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-24-2018 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from Department 206 for Short Cause Trial/Prove Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Eric R. Fleming, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR#5525.

Arkady Itkin, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Ilya Berin, sworn and examined.
No appearance by or on behalf of Defendant Michael Lopez.
Court ordered Defendants DOES 1-100 dismissed without prejudice.
Plaintiff's Trial Brief and exhibits submitted and filed in court.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiff Ilya Berin and against Defendant Michael Lopez as follows:

Principal $88,389.00
Pre-Judgment Interest $0.00
Attorney's Fees $0.00
Costs $630.00
Other: Punitive damages: $200,000.00
Pain and suffering: $200,000.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $489,019.00

Judgment signed in open court.
